title: cirsium acaule subsp. esculentum
intro: Cirsium acaule subsp. esculentum, also known as edible thistle or artichoke thistle, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the western United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant's edible leaves, stems, and roots have been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Cirsium acaule subsp. esculentum can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and capsules, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
